content.problemStatement:
Chef's exam is near. There is a total of MMM subjects in his syllabus. Each subject consists of several topics. However, the questions will be set only from NNN topics. These topics are numbered 111 through NNN. The ithi^{th}ith topic belongs to CithC_i^{th}Cith‚Äč subject and takes TiT_iTi‚Äč hours to cover.

Chef has only KKK hours left before the exam and wants to score the maximum marks. If Chef covers x1x_1x1‚Äč number of topics of the 1st1^{st}1st subject, x2x_2x2‚Äč number of topics of the 2nd2^{nd}2nd subject, and so on upto  xMx_MxM‚Äč number of topics of the MthM^{th}Mth subject in the remaining KKK hours, he will get a total of ‚Ćąx12 ‚ĆČ+‚Ćąx22 ‚ĆČ+‚čĮ+‚ĆąxM2 ‚ĆČ\lceil \frac{x_1}{2} \ \rceil + \lceil \frac{x_2}{2} \ \rceil + \cdots + \lceil \frac{x_M}{2} \ \rceil‚Ćą2x1‚Äč‚Äč ‚ĆČ+‚Ćą2x2‚Äč‚Äč ‚ĆČ+‚čĮ+‚Ćą2xM‚Äč‚Äč ‚ĆČ marks in the exam. So Chef chooses the topics optimally.

Determine the maximum possible marks Chef can score in the exam.

**Note:** ‚Ćąx‚ĆČ\lceil x \rceil‚Ćąx‚ĆČ : Returns the smallest integer that is greater than or equal to xxx (i.e  rounds up to the nearest integer). For example, ‚Ćą1.5‚ĆČ=2\lceil 1.5 \rceil = 2‚Ćą1.5‚ĆČ=2, ‚Ćą5‚ĆČ=5\lceil 5 \rceil = 5‚Ćą5‚ĆČ=5.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains three lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N,M,KN, M, KN,M,K.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \dots, C_NC1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers T1,T2,‚Ä¶,TNT_1, T_2, \dots, T_NT1‚Äč,T2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,TN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the maximum marks Chef can score.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚čÖ1055\cdot10^55‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef covers the 1st1^{st}1st and 2nd2^{nd}2nd topic in 1+1=21 + 1 = 21+1=2 hours. Both topics belongs to 1st1^{st}1st subject. He does not cover any topic of the second subject. By doing so, Chef gets ‚Ćą22¬†‚ĆČ+‚Ćą02¬†‚ĆČ\lceil \frac{2}{2} \ \rceil + \lceil \frac{0}{2} \ \rceil‚Ćą22‚Äč¬†‚ĆČ+‚Ćą20‚Äč¬†‚ĆČ = ‚Ćą¬†1¬†‚ĆČ+‚Ćą¬†0¬†‚ĆČ=1\lceil \ 1 \ \rceil + \lceil \ 0 \ \rceil = 1‚Ćą¬†1¬†‚ĆČ+‚Ćą¬†0¬†‚ĆČ=1 marks.
Test case 222: Chef covers the 1st1^{st}1st topic which belongs to the first subject and 4th4^{th}4th topic which belongs to the second subject in 1+1=21 + 1 = 21+1=2 hours. There is no topic from the third subject. So Chef gets ‚Ćą12¬†‚ĆČ+‚Ćą12¬†‚ĆČ\lceil \frac{1}{2} \ \rceil + \lceil \frac{1}{2} \ \rceil‚Ćą21‚Äč¬†‚ĆČ+‚Ćą21‚Äč¬†‚ĆČ = ‚Ćą¬†0.5¬†‚ĆČ+‚Ćą¬†0.5¬†‚ĆČ=1+1=2\lceil \ 0.5 \ \rceil + \lceil \ 0.5 \ \rceil = 1 + 1 = 2‚Ćą¬†0.5¬†‚ĆČ+‚Ćą¬†0.5¬†‚ĆČ=1+1=2 marks.
